baseline.note: Post-update measured best on the local backend. The HARD invariant is the oracle (G4/G8/G9), which is exact and deterministic; the live floor carries a generous tolerance because the LLM backend is stochastic across model/provider (CI uses claude-haiku). The deterministic reference ceiling is 100. Re-derive the floor after the next full run if the accepted best rises.

cases.desc: The agent's latest authoritative coverage must be >= baseline.coverage_pct - tolerance (G10).
The agent's latest authoritative field-accuracy must be >= baseline.field_accuracy_pct - tolerance (G10).

cases.assert: G4: scoring the gold registry against itself yields quality_score=100 (coverage=100, field-accuracy=100, zero extras).
G4: an empty [] emission scores quality_score=0 — empty output is never credited (the saturation trap is closed).
G4/G5: a single malformed {} object and the all-TC-ERR-sentinel set (the 0/13 collapse) each score quality_score=0; malformed cases are dropped, never counted as covered.

G4: dropping a step (coverage), flipping one involves_* (field-accuracy), blanking an Assert clause (field-accuracy), and inventing an extra case (set-equality) each strictly lower quality_score below 100.
G8: rebuilding the reference registry twice from the manifest is byte-identical.
G9: gold_tc (13) equals the sum of numbered steps over the enabled agent specs; every gold tc_id is reproduced or explained by a logged failure sentinel.
CHANGE 1: an agent whose How section has zero numbered steps (demo-noop) emits [] with NO retry and NO failure sentinel — [] is the correct answer for a zero-step spec.
CHANGE 1: an agent with numbered steps that returns nothing after 3 escalating attempts emits a TC-ERR-<agent> sentinel (reason=extraction_failure, fail=true); never silent, never credited.
